name: Lara Cat Dry Food Junior – Chicken for Kittens 1.9kg
description: With 40% meat and animal-derived ingredients, including 34% chicken, this tasty kibble delivers high-quality protein that closely matches your kitten’s natural nutritional requirements. A carefully balanced combination of calcium, phosphorus, and Vitamin D helps support healthy bone and teeth development, while taurine contributes to healthy vision and heart function. Natural fibres and highly digestible animal proteins promote healthy digestion, helping your kitten absorb nutrients efficiently. Enriched with spirulina, a natural source of beneficial nutrients and antioxidants, this recipe supports vitality and overall health. Made with natural ingredients and free from artificial colourings, Lara Kitten Chicken provides a delicious meal your kitten will enjoy every day. Key Benefits Complete and balanced dry food for kittens Made with delicious chicken for excellent palatability Rich in animal protein to support healthy growth and muscle development Balanced calcium, phosphorus, and Vitamin D for strong bones and teeth Taurine supports healthy vision and heart function Natural fibres help promote healthy digestion Contains spirulina for added nutritional support Made with natural ingredients Free from artificial colourings Suitable for daily feeding Ingredients Cereals, meat and animal by-products (40%, including 34% chicken), plant protein extracts, oils and fats, vegetable by-products (cellulose 1%), vegetables, yeasts, minerals, algae (spirulina 0.1%). Analytical Constituents Crude Protein: 36.0% Crude Fat: 12.0% Crude Fibre: 2.0% Crude Ash: 8.0% Calcium: 1.3% Phosphorus: 1.1% Taurine: 0.3% Nutritional Additives (per kg) Vitamin A: 30,000 IU Vitamin D3: 1,500 IU Vitamin E: 150mg Iron: 120mg Iodine: 2mg Copper: 10mg Manganese: 86mg Zinc: 113mg Selenium: 0.45mg Feeding Guide Kitten 2–3 months: 35–75g daily Kitten 4–6 months: 60–90g daily Kitten 7–9 months: 65–90g daily Kitten 10–12 months: 65–100g daily Always provide fresh drinking water and adjust feeding amounts according to your kitten’s age, activity level, and body condition.
